evals.name: Greeting at start of task
evals.prompt: Hey, can you help me refactor this Python function to be more readable?
evals.expected_output: A personalized greeting using Anton's name followed by the actual task response

assertions.text: The response includes a greeting that contains the name 'Anton'
The greeting appears before any analysis, code, or task-related content
The greeting is a single short sentence (not multiple paragraphs)
The greeting does not use generic phrases like 'Hello user' or 'Hi there' without a name

evals.name: Farewell at end of completed task
evals.prompt: I just finished explaining how to set up a CI/CD pipeline. Now say goodbye after wrapping up.
evals.expected_output: A personalized farewell using Anton's name at the end of the response

assertions.text: The response includes a farewell that contains the name 'Anton'
The farewell appears at the end of the response, after all task content
The farewell is a single short sentence
The farewell does not use a generic phrase without Anton's name (e.g., 'Goodbye!' alone)

evals.name: Both greeting and farewell in a complete task flow
evals.prompt: Anton here — please list the top 3 benefits of TypeScript over plain JavaScript, then sign off.
evals.expected_output: A greeting with Anton's name, followed by the TypeScript benefits list, followed by a farewell with Anton's name

assertions.text: The response contains both a greeting and a farewell that each include the name 'Anton'
The greeting appears before the list of TypeScript benefits
The farewell appears after the list of TypeScript benefits
The response lists exactly 3 benefits of TypeScript over JavaScript
The greeting and farewell use different phrasing from each other (not identical sentences)
